claim: Nathan Lambert writes Interconnects, posting 1–3 times a week on AI models, training methods, and open-source ecosystem; he is a senior research scientist and post-training lead at Allen Institute for AI (Ai2).
quote: 1 to 3 times a week ... over 300 posts to date ... Senior research scientist and post-training lead at the Allen Institute for AI (Ai2)

claim: Jack Clark (Anthropic co-founder, Head of Policy) writes Import AI, a weekly research-focused AI newsletter read by ~70,000 people.
quote: Import AI is a weekly newsletter about artificial intelligence ... read by 70,000 people each week

claim: Zvi Mowshowitz publishes weekly AI updates plus deep model breakdowns (e.g., GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7) on Don't Worry About the Vase, mixing rapid synthesis with long-term world-model building.
quote: weekly AI updates ... doing both speed premium short term updates and long term world model building

claim: Ethan Mollick (Wharton professor) writes One Useful Thing, focused on the implications of AI for work, education, and life from a research-based, applied lens.
quote: Prof. Ethan Mollick focused on trying to understand the implications of AI for work, education, and life

claim: Sebastian Raschka's Ahead of AI is read by 150,000+ researchers and practitioners and is among the most technically rigorous LLM-architecture-focused newsletters on Substack.
quote: more than 150,000 researchers and practitioners ... clear, insightful updates on AI and machine learning

claim: Rohit Krishnan's Strange Loop Canon publishes roughly weekly essays on AI, innovation, and the intersection of technology with economics and business.
quote: the mechanisms driving innovation where technology intersects with economics and business

claim: Charlie Guo joined OpenAI's Developer Experience team in January 2026 and discontinued Artificial Ignorance's news roundups, reducing it as an independent signal source.
quote: I've joined OpenAI ... I'll be discontinuing the news roundups

claim: The Batch (DeepLearning.AI / Andrew Ng) is a weekly team-run AI roundup with a personal letter from Ng covering research, industry, and applied AI for engineers and business leaders.
quote: The Batch presents the most important AI events ... along with a personal note from Andrew Ng

claim: Ben's Bites is a daily AI newsletter founded by Ben Tossell focused on the early-stage AI builder/startup ecosystem with ~120,000 subscribers as of 2026.
quote: Ben's Bites ... go-to resource for the early-stage AI ecosystem, reaching over 120,000 subscribers

claim: Swyx's Latent Space (newsletter + podcast) drew over 10 million readers/listeners in 2025 covering the rise of the AI Engineer; 2026 is positioned around coding agents extending to all knowledge work.
quote: over 10 million readers and listeners came to Latent Space ... 2026 is coding agents breaking containment

claim: Gergely Orosz's The Pragmatic Engineer is the #1 software/AI engineering newsletter on Substack ($15/month or $150/year), covering AI's effect on engineering practice with original survey reporting.
quote: The Pragmatic Engineer is the #1 software/AI engineering newsletter on Substack ... $15/month or $150/year

claim: Simon Willison's newsletter covers LLMs, agents, and open-source data tooling and tracks his prolific weblog output, including a regularly updated 'State of LLMs' talk and Agentic Engineering Patterns guide.
quote: covering AI, LLMs, web engineering, open source, data science, Datasette, SQLite, Python ... State of LLMs, February 2026 edition

claim: Azeem Azhar's Exponential View reaches 100,000+ investors, founders, policymakers and analysts with weekly analysis of how AI and other exponential technologies will shape the next 3–5 years.
quote: Over 100,000 investors, founders, policy-makers, academics and analysts read Azeem's analysis

claim: Eugene Yan (Anthropic) writes a personal newsletter on RecSys, LLMs/AI, and applied ML to 11,800+ readers, blending production-engineering depth with research notes.
quote: sends a newsletter on RecSys, LLMs/AI, and what he's learning to 11,800+ readers
